sources.title: Claude for the legal industry
sources.locator: Product sections on precedents, playbooks, standards, fallback language, setup, risk calibration, and house style
sources.limitation: Vendor product statement; establishes stated capability and positioning, not production compliance.

sources.title: anthropics/claude-for-legal at pinned commit
sources.locator: commercial-legal/skills/cold-start-interview/SKILL.md lines 23-25 and 72-74; commercial-legal/agents/deal-debrief.md lines 89-118; commercial-legal/agents/playbook-monitor.md lines 50-74 and 135-166, all at commit 5ceb305b30b4c82653c9b6642499c12e946ec319
sources.limitation: Public plugin, prompt, connector, and orchestration layer only; closed Anthropic systems are absent.

sources.title: Organize your legal workflows using Projects
sources.locator: Tribal knowledge, firm standards, sample redlines, escalation triggers, shared Projects, and output as infrastructure
sources.limitation: The public registry preserves the access boundary and locator, not a byte-for-byte page capture.

sources.title: Claude for the legal industry: practical deployment guide
sources.locator: Pages 4-7 and 12-15
sources.limitation: Page 15 contains a material internal inconsistency about zero retention.

sources.title: Commercial Terms of Service
sources.locator: Captured HTML: Sections A.2, B, and E covering third-party features, Customer Content rights and model training terms, and confidentiality
sources.limitation: Public standard terms bind only the retrieved text. They do not establish that a particular firm is covered by these terms, its order form or negotiated terms, actual configuration, data flow, access, enforcement, or third-party handling.

sources.title: Data Processing Addendum
sources.locator: Captured HTML: Sections B, F, H, and Schedule 2 on processing scope, audit/reporting, deletion/return terms, and stated security controls
sources.limitation: DPA terms bind only the retrieved text and apply only where the DPA and Customer Personal Data scope govern. They do not prove that a particular firm signed or qualifies for the DPA, actual access logs, actual deletion, or a legal customer's runtime behavior.

sources.title: Is my data used for model training?
sources.locator: Commercial default, feedback, retention, and admin controls
sources.limitation: A captured rendering and a secondary observation disagreed on feedback retention duration; reverify before publication.

sources.title: About the Development Partner Program
sources.locator: Program scope, opt-in/out, notice, retention, and ZDR eligibility
sources.limitation: Disclosed opt-in path; not evidence of default Claude for Legal training.

sources.title: API and data retention
sources.locator: Captured HTML: API and data retention sections, feature eligibility table, stateful products, and ZDR exclusions
sources.limitation: Feature-specific vendor documentation may change. It states documented policy/eligibility conditions, not a particular legal customer's configuration, actual retention, personnel access, data flow, or model-training use.

sources.title: Agent Skills overview
sources.locator: Organizational knowledge, local versus workspace scope, and retention
sources.limitation: Distinguishes local filesystem Skills from uploaded workspace Skills.

sources.title: Freshfields and Anthropic team up to co-build AI legal workflows
sources.locator: Firm-wide co-development, deployment, and connection to institutional knowledge
sources.limitation: Evidence of consensual collaboration, not covert reuse.

sources.title: Clio: privacy-preserving insights into real-world AI use
sources.locator: Method, privacy, and safety-investigation sections
sources.limitation: Supports aggregate usage analysis in defined settings; does not establish customer-specific legal-ontology extraction.

sources.title: anthropics/claude-for-legal public GitHub history
sources.locator: Private local public-API captures: original pulls?state=all&per_page=100 (76 items, SHA-256 940fa06362d0db53f28f710ef48c8fdd503acaf38212257617011315f0a57fc9); issues?state=all&per_page=100 (99 items, SHA-256 19d1d3396197fc12c23715af37e67aca50ba9ad326a801d240b7f5f4cc6a14c6); releases?per_page=100 (0 items, SHA-256 4f53cda18c2baa0c0354bb5f9a3ecbe5ed12ab4d8e11ba873c2f11161202b945). A later dedicated PR-index capture retrieved 2026-07-15T13:35:11Z returned 76 items, HTTP 200, SHA-256 91da08d7e0e89bd62d2b86b39cf8d6c4aad48a4c66c1663b808ffa0a0277b869, and no Link header. For the 14 records with a locally available base-to-merge diff, 14 PR-detail and 14 matching public-commit response bytes were separately captured on 2026-07-15T13:44Z-13:45Z; individual hashes and capture manifests are retained in the ignored SRC-0013 history custody directory.

revision_binding.value: private local public-API captures through 2026-07-15T13:35:11Z; member hashes and retrieval bounds recorded in locator

sources.limitation: A bounded unauthenticated public-API capture does not prove deleted, private, permission-dependent, subsequently changed, or unavailable history. It is distinct from the pinned code revision and does not establish strategy, intent, or runtime behavior.

sources.title: Law firm Kirkland to spend $500 million developing its own AI platform
sources.locator: Reuters public article title and publication date; exact URL observed 2026-07-15
sources.limitation: Secondary reporting and public metadata only; no archived article bytes or primary Kirkland architecture record. It cannot support a promoted custody, confidentiality, or competitive-effect claim.

sources.title: Kirkland & Ellis to spend $500mn building its own AI technology
sources.locator: Financial Times public article title and publication date; Kirkland public in-the-news page independently identifies the same Financial Times article
sources.limitation: Secondary reporting with public metadata; article body may be paywalled and was not archived. It cannot support a promoted custody, confidentiality, or competitive-effect claim.

sources.title: Kirkland & Ellis and Palantir to build AI tool to assist private equity firms
sources.locator: Financial Times public article title and publication date; exact URL observed 2026-07-15
sources.limitation: Secondary reporting with public metadata; article body may be paywalled and was not archived. It cannot support a promoted custody, confidentiality, or competitive-effect claim.

sources.title: Claude for Financial Services
sources.locator: Financial Analysis Solution, connectors, use cases, underwriting, compliance, and internal-data integration; updated 2026-04-10
sources.limitation: Vendor product statement evidences offered integrations and positioning, not a customer's executed controls or secondary use.

sources.title: Advancing Claude in healthcare and the life sciences
sources.locator: Healthcare connectors, prior authorization, records, coding, claims appeals, clinical-trial operations, and regulatory workflows
sources.limitation: Vendor statement does not establish any undisclosed collection, training, or customer-specific ontology transfer.

sources.title: DXC will integrate Claude into the systems banks, airlines, and other regulated industries rely on
sources.locator: Lines describing forward-deployed engineers, mission-critical systems, insurance, cybersecurity, application services, banks, airlines, manufacturers, and government
sources.limitation: Partnership announcement states planned and current work; it does not prove a particular customer's deployment or provider intent.

sources.title: UST is bringing Claude to physical AI
sources.locator: Engineering validation, schematics, regression tests, digital twins, factories, healthcare, telecom, and banking workflows
sources.limitation: Joint vendor statement; reported performance and controls require independent verification for promoted claims.

sources.title: OpenAI Frontier
sources.locator: Business Context, durable institutional memory, systems of record, evaluation loops, forward-deployed engineering, and industry examples
sources.limitation: Product positioning evidences market direction, not covert capture or any customer's actual configuration.

sources.title: Travelers deploys AI-powered claims countrywide with OpenAI
sources.locator: Claims intake, policy questions, claims infrastructure, orchestration systems, and internal tools
sources.limitation: Joint customer story supports the described workflow only; it does not establish provider-side retention, training, or ownership.

sources.title: OpenAI and PwC collaborate to reimagine the office of the CFO
sources.locator: Planning, forecasting, reporting, procurement, payments, treasury, tax, close, controls, and customer-zero workflow validation
sources.limitation: Partnership and provider statement; planned workflows and reported results need independent verification for higher-grade claims.

sources.title: Claude for Creative Work
sources.locator: Creative-tool connectors, cross-tool pipelines, iterative feedback, 3D/design/audio/video production, and interoperability
sources.limitation: Product statement describes capabilities and integrations, not ownership of creative workflows or assets.

sources.title: The Open Source Definition
sources.locator: Clause 6, no discrimination against fields of endeavor
sources.limitation: Defines OSI open source; it is not individualized legal advice.

sources.title: Apache License, Version 2.0
sources.locator: Sections 2-6 and application appendix
sources.limitation: The repository owner must determine rights ownership and correct application; this source is not legal advice.

sources.title: MPL 2.0 FAQ
sources.locator: File-level copyleft, larger works, distribution obligations, and license application
sources.limitation: Explanatory FAQ is not the license text or individualized legal advice.

sources.title: Creative Commons Frequently Asked Questions
sources.locator: Software recommendation, NonCommercial and ShareAlike elements, databases, rights limits, and legal-advice disclaimer
sources.limitation: Guidance is not legal advice and CC terms apply only to rights the licensor controls.

sources.title: Developer Certificate of Origin 1.1
sources.locator: Right-to-submit certification and public indefinite contribution record
sources.limitation: DCO is a contributor attestation, not copyright assignment or a guarantee of provenance.

sources.title: GitHub Terms of Service
sources.locator: Sections D.4-D.9 and J.3 on public content, repository contributions, platform licenses, and AI-related use
sources.limitation: Platform terms may change and account-specific enterprise agreements may differ; obtain legal advice for hosting decisions.

sources.title: Best practices for using the REST API
sources.locator: Polling avoidance, conditional requests, rate limits, retries, and backoff
sources.limitation: Operational guidance may change; reverify before enabling a watcher.

sources.title: Secure use reference for GitHub Actions
sources.locator: Untrusted input, token permissions, action pinning, and privileged workflow risks
sources.limitation: Operational guidance may change; threat-model any workflow before activation.

sources.title: ABA Model Rule 1.6: Confidentiality of Information
sources.locator: Rule 1.6(a)-(c) and comments 3-4, 18-19
sources.limitation: The Model Rules are models, not jurisdiction-specific law; adoption and interpretation vary.

sources.title: ABA Formal Opinion 512: Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools
sources.publisher: American Bar Association Standing Committee on Ethics and Professional Responsibility
sources.locator: Sections on competence, confidentiality, communication, supervision, candor, and fees
sources.limitation: General ethics guidance under the Model Rules; fact-specific and not jurisdiction-specific legal advice.

sources.title: ABA Model Rule 5.4: Professional Independence of a Lawyer
sources.locator: Rule 5.4(a)-(d) and professional-independence comments
sources.limitation: Ownership and fee-sharing rules vary by jurisdiction; this model rule cannot support a nationwide categorical conclusion.

sources.title: Find a Case (PACER)
sources.publisher: Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts
sources.locator: Electronic case files, dockets, opinions, historical records, and access terms
sources.limitation: PACER coverage does not include every state matter, every historical record, sealed material, unfiled work, or internal deliberation.

sources.title: What information is available through PACER?
sources.publisher: Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts
sources.locator: Public case and docket information plus stated exclusions for sealed and restricted materials
sources.limitation: Public availability does not make the record complete, representative, or a source of internal strategy.

sources.title: ABA Model Rule 1.18: Duties to Prospective Client, Comment
sources.locator: Comments on limiting initial consultation and duties arising from prospective-client information
sources.limitation: The Model Rules are models and the existence and scope of duties are fact- and jurisdiction-dependent.

sources.title: ABA Model Rule 5.3: Responsibilities Regarding Nonlawyer Assistance
sources.locator: Managerial and supervisory duties for nonlawyer assistance
sources.limitation: The rule is a model and does not substitute for governing jurisdictional rules or a fact-specific vendor review.

sources.title: Arizona Alternative Business Structure Program
sources.locator: Program definition, licensing, regulation, and nonlawyer economic interest or decision authority
sources.limitation: One jurisdiction's regulated program does not authorize a structure or practice in other jurisdictions and does not dissolve client duties.

sources.title: Palantir Ontology
sources.locator: Ontology as enterprise data, logic, action, security, and Human+AI decision orchestration
sources.limitation: Vendor product positioning supports the control-plane comparison; it does not prove custody, access, or behavior in any law-firm deployment.

sources.title: AlphaGo at 10: How AI Innovation Is Paving the Path to AGI
sources.locator: A creative spark section, especially Move 37 in Game 2 and the delayed strategic value described at page lines 140-144 in the 2026-07-14 observation
sources.limitation: DeepMind describes its own system and a closed game; this does not prove analogous legal creativity or capability.

sources.title: Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search
sources.locator: Nature 529, 484-489; DOI 10.1038/nature16961; architecture, expert-game supervision, reinforcement learning, and tree-search method
sources.limitation: The paper predates the Lee Sedol match and establishes AlphaGo's technical foundation, not transfer to open-ended professional reasoning.

sources.title: Mastering the game of Go without human knowledge
sources.locator: Abstract and Figures 1-6; Nature 550, 354-359; DOI 10.1038/nature24270
sources.limitation: AlphaGo Zero operated in a closed game with known rules and a clean reward; performance without human game data does not establish the same result in law.

sources.title: Algorithmic progress in language models
sources.locator: Abstract, methodology, and benchmark-threshold estimates across more than 200 language-model evaluations from 2012-2023; arXiv 2403.05812
sources.limitation: Efficiency estimates are benchmark-, model-, dataset-, and period-specific and do not justify indefinite exponential growth or predict legal capability.

sources.title: Artificial intelligence, data and competition
sources.locator: Updated 62-page PDF; OECD Artificial Intelligence Papers No. 18; DOI 10.1787/e7e88884-en; page 2 update note and sections on customer-held data, specialised industry data, feedback effects, concentration, and market tipping
sources.limitation: The original 24 May 2024 version was updated in place; this hash binds the reviewed updated PDF. Competition-policy analysis identifies possible pathways and uncertain market effects; it does not prove law-firm data use by a named provider.

sources.title: Knowledge Transfer: A Basis for Competitive Advantage in Firms
sources.publisher: Organizational Behavior and Human Decision Processes
sources.locator: Volume 82, Issue 1, pages 150-169; DOI 10.1006/obhd.2000.2893; knowledge embedded in members, tools, tasks, and interaction networks
sources.limitation: Organizational-learning theory is not specific to law firms or foundation models; application to attorney brain trusts is a reasoned analogy.

sources.title: Predictors of IT Support for Knowledge Management in the Professions: An Empirical Study of Law Firms in Norway
sources.locator: Abstract and study design; Volume 15, Issue 1, pages 69-78; DOI 10.1177/026839620001500107
sources.limitation: The study concerns Norwegian law firms in 2000 and IT-supported knowledge management, not frontier-model training or current global large-law competition.

sources.title: Group Boycotts
sources.locator: Independent refusal-to-deal boundary and competitor agreements not to deal or to deal only on agreed terms
sources.limitation: General federal enforcement guidance is not legal advice, is fact-specific, may change, and does not resolve every standard-setting or professional-association arrangement.

sources.title: Claude for the legal industry
sources.locator: Captured HTML: sections describing legal plugins, MCP connectors, and customization to playbooks, precedent, and house style
sources.limitation: A private local capture binds only the retrieved vendor response. It can evidence stated capabilities and integration framing, not a customer deployment, model-training use, telemetry, retention, provider ownership, or intent.

sources.title: Deploying Claude across the legal industry
sources.locator: Captured HTML: sections describing matter-level work, connectors, skills, subagents, plugins, playbooks, precedent, house style, and vendor-stated control topics
sources.limitation: A private local capture binds only the retrieved vendor response. It can evidence stated capabilities and integration framing, not a particular firm’s configuration, data flow, retention, training, access, or contractual terms.

sources.title: MCP connector
sources.locator: Captured HTML: remote MCP server configuration, OAuth, multi-server use, and data-exchange retention statements
sources.limitation: A vendor documentation capture can evidence the documented MCP connector interface and stated terms at retrieval time, not a legal-product deployment, a particular firm's exchanged data, third-party server practices, provider access, or training use.

sources.title: Web Annotation Data Model
sources.locator: W3C Recommendation; selectors, bodies, targets, lifecycle, and JSON representation
sources.limitation: URL and primary-source metadata are registered; no local byte capture was made in this tranche.

sources.title: PROV Overview
sources.locator: W3C provenance family overview; Entity, Activity, Agent, derivation, generation, and attribution
sources.limitation: PROV supplies interchange concepts, not this project's epistemic or argument authority.

sources.title: Journal Article Tag Suite 1.4
sources.locator: ANSI/NISO Z39.96-2024 overview and current JATS 1.4 resources
sources.limitation: JATS describes article interchange and publication structure, not evidence truth or review status.

sources.title: RO-Crate Metadata Specification 1.3
sources.locator: Recommendation; file-based research objects, JSON-LD entities, provenance, workflows, and profiles
sources.limitation: A crate packages research objects but does not replace paragraph-level claim genealogy.

sources.title: Nanopublication Guidelines
sources.locator: Assertion, assertion provenance, and publication-information structure
sources.limitation: The current guidelines are community guidance rather than a formal standards-body standard.

sources.title: Manubot: Manuscripts, open and automated
sources.locator: Markdown and Git authoring, identifier citations, and HTML/PDF/DOCX build workflow
sources.limitation: Project documentation establishes intended workflow features, not fitness for this repository's evidence gates.

sources.title: Argdown
sources.locator: Argument syntax, support/attack/undercut relations, tags, maps, and exports
sources.limitation: Argdown is an authoring and visualization language; imported relations are not reviewed evidence.

sources.title: Argument Interchange Format specification
sources.locator: Information nodes and scheme nodes for inference, conflict, and preference
sources.limitation: The research specification does not include this project's review, freshness, publication, or source-hash controls.

sources.title: Citation Typing Ontology
sources.locator: Typed citation intents and inverse relationships
sources.limitation: CiTO vocabulary does not itself verify the truth or evidentiary sufficiency of a citation relation.

sources.title: Assisting in Writing Wikipedia-like Articles From Scratch with Large Language Models
sources.locator: STORM prewriting, perspective discovery, question asking, source-grounded conversations, and outline generation
sources.limitation: A research prototype for Wikipedia-like writing does not prove legal-paper accuracy or safe claim promotion.

sources.title: Language agents achieve superhuman synthesis of scientific knowledge
sources.locator: PaperQA2, LitQA2, iterative search, citation traversal, evidence ranking, and synthesis evaluation
sources.limitation: Reported performance is configuration- and benchmark-dependent and does not authorize automatic acceptance of claims or citations.

sources.title: PRISMA-S: Reporting Literature Searches
sources.locator: Sixteen-item literature-search reporting extension and supporting materials
sources.limitation: This project borrows search-receipt discipline but does not claim its exploratory scan is a PRISMA systematic review.
